content: bd create uses --description or -d for the description flag, NOT --desc. Title is a positional arg: bd create 'title' --description 'desc' --priority P2

agent-busy marker pattern: .legio/agent-busy/{agentName} file presence indicates agent is actively executing a tool. Absence means idle. Used by mail auto-nudge smart push delivery to decide whether to call nudgeAgent() directly or only write pending marker.

Smart push delivery in mail send: when shouldNudge is true, always write pending nudge marker first (reliable path), then check isAgentIdle(). If idle, call nudgeAgent(cwd, to, '[mail from X] reason: subject', true) with force=true to skip debounce. nudgeAgent is non-fatal (returns delivered:false on failure, never throws).

bin/legio.mjs shim: use register('tsx/esm', import.meta.url) not register('tsx/esm', pathToFileURL('./')) — pathToFileURL('./') resolves relative to CWD which breaks global installs; import.meta.url is always the shim file's own URL

Agent busy-state tracking pattern in log.ts: tool-start creates .legio/agent-busy/{agentName} file (non-fatal try/catch), tool-end removes it (non-fatal, file may not exist). Used by watchdog/monitor to detect in-flight tool executions without querying SQLite.

waitForTuiReady pattern: polls tmux capture-pane -p until pane has non-empty content, replaces hardcoded sleep(3000) before sendKeys beacon. Default timeout 15s, interval 500ms. Returns Promise<void>, warns stderr on timeout but never throws. Callers proceed regardless.

sendKeys error differentiation: parse stderr for 'no server running' (AgentError: Tmux server not running) vs 'session not found'/'can't find session' (AgentError: Session not found) vs other (generic AgentError with stderr). Callers pattern-match on err.message.

Commit messages containing 'git push' (the two words together) trigger the PreToolUse Bash danger guard, blocking the commit. Write commit messages that avoid 'git push' literally — use 'remote-block', 'push to remote', or similar phrasing instead.

hooks.json.tmpl push-guard pattern: the PreToolUse Bash entry uses ENV_GUARD + read stdin + sed extraction of command field + grep -qE for git push pattern. Does NOT use {{AGENT_NAME}} — agent name not needed for simple push blocking.

PostToolUse mulch diff hook pattern: matcher 'Bash', reads stdin, extracts command field via sed, checks grep -qE for git commit pattern, then runs 'mulch diff HEAD~1' to show what changed in the commit. Enables automatic diff capture after each commit.

TaskDetailView route pattern: #task/:taskId uses parseHash() which splits on '/', so param=taskId. Router case 'task' passes taskId prop to TaskDetailView. Reached via IssueCard onClick (not NAV_LINKS).

IssueCard click-through change: onClick now sets location.hash = 'task/' + issue.id (not '#task/...' — hash prefix is optional in assignment). Removed appState/pendingChatContext import as it was the only use.

zombie reconciliation fix: do NOT call updateLastActivity() after updateState('zombie') in coordinator.ts and gateway.ts — the SQL CASE in updateLastActivity transitions zombie->working, immediately overriding the state. Only call updateState when marking zombie; skip updateLastActivity in that code path.

inferDomainsFromFiles() in src/mulch/client.ts maps file paths to mulch domains using glob patterns. DEFAULT_DOMAIN_MAP maps src/commands/**->cli, src/server/routes*->server, src/server/public/**->frontend, src/sessions/**->server, src/metrics/**->server, src/events/**->server, src/mail/**->server, src/agents/**->swarm, src/worktree/**->swarm+merge, src/merge/**->merge, src/beads/**->cli, src/*.test.*->testing, src/**/*.test.*->testing, agents/**->swarm, templates/**->swarm. sling.ts calls inferDomainsFromFiles(fileScope) and passes inferred domains to overlay mulchDomains and mulch.prime().

LegioConfig.mulch.domainMap is an optional Record<string, string[]> for custom file-glob-to-domain mapping. When empty or absent, inferDomainsFromFiles falls back to DEFAULT_DOMAIN_MAP. Config default is {} (empty object). Made optional (not required) to avoid breaking existing test fixtures that construct LegioConfig without domainMap.

inferDomainsFromFiles pattern: DEFAULT_DOMAIN_MAP (exported) maps glob patterns to domain arrays; matchGlob() (internal) converts glob to regex handling ** (zero-or-more segments) and * (single-segment); inferDomainsFromFiles(files, domainMap?) returns deduplicated domain array. All three live in src/mulch/client.ts above createMulchClient.

sling domain inference pattern: compute inferredDomains from fileScope before the mulchExpertise block; pass inferredDomains to mulch.prime() when non-empty (else fall back to --files); use inferredDomains for overlayConfig.mulchDomains when non-empty (else fall back to config.mulch.domains). Avoids double-call by computing once and reusing.

stdout-before-beacon pattern: gateway.ts, coordinator.ts, supervisor.ts must write JSON/text output to stdout immediately after store.upsert(), BEFORE the blocking beacon sleep+sendKeys operations. When callers pipe stdout (e.g. runLegio), tmux child inherits the pipe FD so sleeping first keeps the pipe open and hangs the caller.

coordinator.ts early JSON output uses watchdog:false, monitor:false defaults since output is written before those operations complete. Non-JSON supplementary messages (watchdog/monitor PID) remain in-place after their operations.
supervisor.ts store.upsert() must happen BEFORE beacon sendKeys (same as coordinator/gateway pattern) so hook-triggered updateLastActivity() can find the session entry.

parseTranscriptTexts in transcript.ts uses a line-offset watermark (nextLine) for incremental JSONL parsing. Trailing empty from split('\n') is excluded from nextLine: nextLine = lastLine.trim().length === 0 ? lines.length - 1 : lines.length.

session-end chat sync pattern for persistent agents: resolve transcript via .transcript-path cache, read .chat-transcript-offset watermark, call parseTranscriptTexts(path, fromLine), filter to assistant messages, insert into chat.db coordinator session via createChatStore, then writeFile offset. Non-fatal try-catch wraps entire block.

POST /api/coordinator/chat persists user messages to SQLite (chat.db), forwards to coordinator via sendKeys with newline-flattening (→ space) to prevent embedded Enter in Claude Code TUI input handling

Daemon spawn env hygiene: when server.ts spawns a detached daemon, strip __LEGIO_TSX_LOADED from child env (const childEnv: NodeJS.ProcessEnv = { ...process.env, LEGIO_SERVER_DAEMON: '1' }; delete childEnv.__LEGIO_TSX_LOADED) so bin/legio.mjs re-execs with --import tsx. On Node v23+, the shim cannot strip types without tsx active, but children inherit the parent env var and skip re-exec.

bin/legio.mjs shim hardening for node_modules: check process.execArgv for '--import tsx' in addition to __LEGIO_TSX_LOADED. When inNodeModules=true, only skip re-exec when tsxImportActive is also true. This prevents ERR_UNSUPPORTED_NODE_MODULES_TYPE_STRIPPING on Node v23+ when daemon children inherit __LEGIO_TSX_LOADED=1 but tsx is not active.

biome.json excludes .legio/ directory — cannot run biome check on files inside .legio/worktrees/* from the repo root. Builders must run biome from within their worktree cwd. Post-merge lint fixes must run after code is on a non-.legio/ path.

README.md Agent Types table should include all 10 agent roles: Coordinator, Supervisor, Gateway, CTO, Scout, Builder, Reviewer, Lead, Merger, Monitor. Gateway and CTO were added alongside the 8 original roles.

collectProviderEnv() in src/config.ts collects ANTHROPIC_BASE_URL and ANTHROPIC_AUTH_TOKEN from process.env, returning only set (non-empty) values as Record<string,string> suitable for tmux createSession() env overrides.

ModelAlias = 'sonnet' | 'opus' | 'haiku' is defined in src/types.ts and used for LegioConfig.models values and AgentDefinition.model. ModelRef = ModelAlias | string allows provider-qualified strings. ProviderConfig interface holds optional baseUrl and authToken.

Never use 'legio worktree clean --all' during active sessions — it nukes ALL worktrees including actively running agents. Use '--completed' to only clean finished agents, or target specific agents by name.

Ideas CRUD API (GET/POST /api/ideas, PUT/DELETE /api/ideas/:id, POST /api/ideas/:id/dispatch, POST /api/ideas/:id/backlog) replaces strategy.json routes. Data stored in .legio/ideas.json with {ideas: Idea[]} format.

description: Issue duplication between coordinator and leads: coordinator creates subtask issues then spawns a lead who creates its own subtasks. Builders close lead issues but coordinator issues are orphaned.
resolution: Only create the parent issue at coordinator level. Let leads decompose into their own subtasks. Do not pre-create subtask issues.

description: stop-cmd-lead ran legio worktree clean --all, nuking its own worktree and sibling agents. Lead agents should not have access to --all flag.
resolution: Leads must never run legio worktree clean --all. Add to lead agent definition constraints. Leads should only clean their own sub-worker worktrees by name.

content: legio stop command uses DI pattern for tmux (StopDeps._tmux) matching coordinator.ts pattern; enables testing without real tmux sessions
legio stop sorts active sessions deepest-first (b.depth - a.depth) before killing, ensuring leaf workers are stopped before parents
coordinator stop path always runs tryStopWatchdog + tryCleanHeadlessPid + tryCompleteRun regardless of whether coordinator tmux session was alive
Leads should nudge builders to commit once their specific tests pass rather than waiting for the full vitest suite, which takes 3-5min and can block due to TaskOutput hook restrictions.

sling dispatch mail race fix: write dispatch mail to mail.db (step 11b) BEFORE createSession() call (step 12) to guarantee mail exists when agent's SessionStart hook fires legio mail check. Without this, agent boots, checks mail, finds nothing, idles without assignment.

buildAutoDispatch() pure function pattern in sling.ts: returns {from, to, subject, body, type, priority} object; from defaults to 'orchestrator' when parentAgent is null; insert into mail store uses type cast ('dispatch' as 'dispatch') and requires threadId: null field to satisfy MailMessage Omit type.

gateway is a persistent capability: must be in PERSISTENT_CAPABILITIES in both src/commands/log.ts AND src/watchdog/health.ts. Gateway runs at depth 0, has long idle periods waiting for chat/mail, and should not be flagged stale/zombie based on lastActivity.

waitForTuiReady TUI marker pattern: use TUI_READY_MARKERS const (box-drawing chars + 'bypass permissions') and hasTuiMarkers() helper exported from tmux.ts. Check content.includes(marker) rather than content.trim().length > 0 to avoid shell-prompt false positives. postReadyDelay option (default 500ms) added as defense in depth after marker detection.

waitForTuiReady TUI marker detection: use TUI_READY_MARKERS const (box-drawing chars ─ ━ ╭ ╰ + 'bypass permissions') and hasTuiMarkers() helper instead of content.trim().length > 0 to avoid false-positive ready detection on shell prompts. postReadyDelay option (default 500ms) provides defense in depth after marker detection.

trace.ts (lines 33-43) defines canonical CLI arg parsing helpers: getFlag(args, flag) returns string|undefined, hasFlag(args, flag) returns boolean. All query/control commands should follow this pattern instead of custom parseArgs/parseFlags.

All observability/query commands should support --json output mode for machine readability: trace, status, logs, errors, feed, replay, costs, metrics, inspect, doctor, run. Commands missing it: watch.ts, worktree.ts. Check clean.ts, init.ts for incomplete implementation.

Help text formatting: Command title line should use em dash (—) separator, not dashes (--). Inconsistent across: trace.ts:196 (dashes), status.ts:275 (em dash), down.ts:57 (em dash), logs.ts:428 (dashes). Standardize all to: 'legio command — Description'

legio down gateway stop: gateway is stopped as step 2 between coordinator (step 1) and server (step 3) in down.ts. gatewayStopped is included in nothingToStop check and JSON output. Non-zero exit from gateway stop is non-fatal (same pattern as coordinator).

Arg parsing helpers should follow trace.ts canonical pattern: getFlag() returns flag value or undefined, hasFlag() checks boolean presence. Non-compliant: prime.ts (custom parseArgs), supervisor.ts (custom parseFlags), hooks.ts (args.includes), group.ts (custom getPositionalArgs)

hooks-deployer path boundary guards have a memory dir exception: both buildPathBoundaryGuardScript and buildBashPathBoundaryScript allow writes to $HOME/.claude/projects/*/memory/** so agents can update their auto-memory directory without triggering PATH_BOUNDARY_VIOLATION
watch.ts watchdog daemon spawn: use process.execPath (node) with --import tsx flag. Pattern: spawn(process.execPath, ["--import", "tsx", legioBin, ...args])

trace.ts defines canonical CLI arg parsing pattern: getFlag(args, flag) returns flag value or undefined, hasFlag(args, flag) returns boolean. Commands like agents, sling, merge, trace follow this pattern; others (prime, coordinator, supervisor) use custom parseArgs/parseFlags functions reducing consistency.

SQLite configuration consistency: All database stores should configure PRAGMA journal_mode=WAL, synchronous=NORMAL, busy_timeout=5000 for predictable concurrent access from multiple agent processes. mail/store.ts, sessions/store.ts, events/store.ts, merge/queue.ts, audit-store.ts follow this pattern correctly; metrics/store.ts (line 46) and init.ts (lines 169, 177) are missing synchronous=NORMAL pragma, should be added for consistency.

All spawn() calls in legio use array argument pattern [command, [...args]] never shell string interpolation. This pattern at spawn callsites in 27 files prevents all command injection vectors. Verified in worktree/manager.ts, commands/merge.ts, commands/sling.ts, server/routes.ts and others.

name: Event timeline polling
description: feed command uses default 1000ms interval (min 200ms) for --follow mode to continuously fetch getTimeline() results. Dashboard uses 2000ms default, inspect uses 3000ms default. Watchdog daemon uses 500ms for process health checks. These intervals are well-tuned for their respective tools and appropriately balance responsiveness vs system load.

content: All 34 CLI commands support --help via args.includes('--help') or args.includes('-h'), output formatted with em-dash separator (e.g., 'legio prime — Load context...'). Help text defined in HELP constant and written via process.stdout.write(). Pattern consistent across all commands.

Query/observability commands should support --json output for machine readability: trace, status, inspect, clean, doctor, feed, errors, replay, run, costs, metrics, server, up, down, stop all have --json support. Commands: init, prime, dashboard, watch (intentionally no --json). Issue: supervisor.ts declares --json in completions.ts but may not implement output.

name: Composite timeline indexes
description: Events table uses composite indexes on (agent_name, created_at) and (run_id, created_at) for efficient timeline queries with built-in ordering. Mail table uses (to_agent, read) composite index for inbox queries. These patterns enable O(log n) lookups with natural ordering for chronological event feeds. Partial indexes like idx_events_level_error ON events(level) WHERE level='error' are effective for filtering hot subsets.

content: Error type hierarchy uses 11 custom error classes (ConfigError, AgentError, HierarchyError, WorktreeError, MailError, MergeError, ValidationError, GroupError, LifecycleError, ServerError) all extending LegioError base. Each includes semantic code field (CONFIG_ERROR, AGENT_ERROR) and context metadata (path, branch, agent) for programmatic handling.

CLAUDE.md command count: index.ts entry point comment says '34 commands' (was 29). Five commands added: gateway, server, up, down, stop. Two agents added: gateway.md (planning companion, no worktree, depth 0) and cto.md (strategic analyst, outputs strategy.json). Total agent count is now 10.

Dead export cleanup pattern in types.ts: protocol payload types (MergeReadyPayload, MergedPayload, etc.) only need export if directly imported by consumers. When only referenced via MailPayloadMap indexed access, export keyword can be removed safely — TypeScript resolves indexed types from non-exported members.

CTO strategy types (StrategyPriority, StrategyEffort, StrategyStatus, StrategyRecommendation, StrategyFile) and AgentLayers interface were dead exports — CTO agent feature never fully implemented. These were safely deleted.

resolveParentAgent() helper pattern in sling.ts: export a small helper that resolves parentAgent from --parent flag ?? LEGIO_AGENT_NAME env var ?? null, keeping slingCommand simple and the logic independently unit-testable

watchdog recovery zombie fix: after calling transitionState() (which writes zombie) before attemptRecovery(), successful recovery must explicitly call store.updateState(agentName, 'completed') in the else branch to override the zombie state. Without this, successful recovery leaves the agent in zombie state in the SessionStore.

Watchdog recovery pattern in daemon.ts: attemptRecovery() checks checkpoint via loadCheckpoint, reads file-based recovery-count at .legio/agents/{name}/recovery-count, then spawns legio sling with args from checkpoint (beadId, capability, filesModified, parentAgent, depth). Returns { recovered: boolean }. Recovery wired into both terminate paths: direct (check.action===terminate) and escalation level 3+ case. For direct terminate, transitionState already sets zombie before recovery runs — the distinction is whether store.updateEscalation is reset. For escalation level 3, terminated: !recovered controls whether store.updateState(zombie) is called.

legio up gateway start pattern: after server start (step 4), check gateway status via 'legio gateway status --json', parse JSON in try/catch to handle both JSON.parse exceptions and non-zero exit codes, start with 'legio gateway start --no-attach' if not running. Gateway failure is non-fatal (stderr warning, continue). Track gatewayStarted/gatewayAlreadyRunning flags in JSON output.

legio up gateway start: try/catch wraps entire gateway status block (run + JSON.parse) so both parse exceptions and non-zero exit codes fall through to gatewayRunning=false, triggering gateway start attempt. Non-fatal: stderr warning on start failure, never throws.

validateHierarchy in sling.ts: scout capability is allowed without --parent (same as lead). The condition is: parentAgent === null && capability \!== 'lead' && capability \!== 'scout'. This enables the coordinator to directly spawn scouts for exploration tasks.

checkParentAgentLimit and checkDuplicateLead are pure functions exported from sling.ts; they take ReadonlyArray session snapshots and throw AgentError on violation. checkParentAgentLimit is only called when parentAgent is not null.
When adding optional config fields to LegioConfig (e.g. maxAgentsPerLead?: number), use ?? fallback at call site for backward compat with existing config.yaml files that predate the field.

When adding a new required field to LegioConfig interface, use optional (?) to avoid breaking inline test fixtures across doctor/test files outside the agent's scope. Use a ?? fallback at the call site in sling.ts to apply the default.

When running bun run lint from a worktree under .legio/, biome ignores all files (biome.json excludes !**/.legio). Lint must be verified by visual inspection in worktree context; run from canonical repo only works for files outside .legio/.

generateOverlay resolves template via import.meta.dirname, reading from the worktree copy of templates/overlay.md.tmpl (not canonical repo). Tests calling generateOverlay directly work because the worktree has all non-modified template files.

--skip-review flag in sling.ts: parsed as args.includes('--skip-review'), stored as skipReview?: boolean in OverlayConfig, passed to overlayConfig object. generateOverlay inserts '## Dispatch Overrides' section before '## Expertise' heading using string slice insertion (not template placeholder) when config.skipReview is true.

name: Type union member removal cascade
description: Removing a type union member (e.g. AgentState 'stalled') causes cascade failures in all switch/case statements and comparisons across the codebase. When planning type-level removals, scope must include all consumers of the type, not just the definition file and primary usage sites.

content: legio sling --skip-review flag adds Dispatch Overrides section to lead overlays via overlay.ts; leads must check for this section to skip reviewer spawn.

sling.ts has three spawn guards: checkParentAgentLimit (maxAgentsPerLead, default 5), checkDuplicateLead (prevents two leads on same task), and validateHierarchy (coordinator can only spawn leads/scouts).

README section order after restructure: tagline intro → Why Legio? (problem/solution/payoff) → See It in Action (3 screenshots) → Quick Start → How It Works → Web UI → Requirements → Installation → CLI Reference → REST API → Tech Stack → Development → Project Structure → License

When biome auto-fix is needed after a builder completes, spawn a lint-fix builder that first pulls the builder's branch then runs bunx biome check --write. The lint-fix branch becomes the merge target (superseding the original builder branch).

Lead dispatch mail body must avoid literal shell patterns that match the PreToolUse hook regex (e.g. 'git merge', 'cat >>', 'mkdir'). Rephrase instructions to avoid these patterns: use 'pull branch X into yours' instead of 'git merge X'.
biome import order: when adding new project-relative imports, always append them AFTER existing project-relative imports (by path), not before. Inserting before breaks biome organizeImports.
biome formatter collapses short arrow callbacks to single line; multi-line mockImplementation calls in tests trigger formatter errors when the body fits within 100-char width.

pipe-pane streaming pattern in tmux.ts: startPipePane(sessionName, logPath) runs 'tmux pipe-pane -t session cat >> logPath', stopPipePane(sessionName) runs 'tmux pipe-pane -t session' with no command to close pipe. readTerminalLog(logPath, tailLines?) returns string|null (null when file missing). killSession calls stopPipePane before kill-session. sling.ts calls startPipePane after waitForTuiReady in non-fatal try-catch. inspect.ts and routes.ts prefer terminal log over capture-pane with fallback.

rowToSession backward compat pattern: use conditional spread for optional new columns so existing toEqual tests don't break. Example: ...(row.terminal_log_path \!== null ? { terminalLogPath: row.terminal_log_path } : {}). Omitting null fields preserves shape equality for tests that create AgentSession objects without the new field.

gatherStatus run-aware filtering: reads current-run.txt with try/catch fallback, uses getByRunIncludeOrphans(runId) when present, getActive() when absent; change is in src/commands/status.ts around line 82

When renaming a LegioConfig field (e.g. beads -> taskTracker), keep the old field as optional (?) in types.ts for backward compat with out-of-scope test fixtures that use inline config objects. Consumers use optional chaining (config.taskTracker?.backend ?? 'auto'). Add a migration function (migrateDeprecatedXKey) in config.ts that converts old key to new key at parse time.

Tracker abstraction integration pattern: sling.ts and supervisor.ts use createTrackerClient(config.taskTracker?.backend ?? 'auto', projectRoot); server/routes.ts always uses createTrackerClient('auto', projectRoot) since routes don't load config for tracker settings.

up.ts gateway start uses fire-and-forget spawnDetached (detached+stdio:ignore+unref) instead of blocking runCommand; DI via _spawnDetached in UpDeps. This avoids ~3.5s hang from gateway's beacon sleep.

legio up spawnDetached pattern: gateway start uses spawnDetached() (detached+stdio:ignore+unref) instead of runCommand() to avoid ~3.5s hang. DI hook _spawnDetached?: (cmd, opts?) => void added to UpDeps for testing. Tests verify gateway start goes through _spawnDetached, NOT _runCommand.
